message:

 {"id":"beads_rust-kr5i","title":"E2E tests: label command","description":"# E2E Tests for `label` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br label add <issue_id> <label>` - Add label to issue
- `br label remove <issue_id> <label>` - Remove label from issue
- `br label list` - List all labels in workspace
- `br label --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single label, verify via show
2. Add multiple labels to same issue
3. Remove label, verify removed
4. List all labels across issues
5. Add same label to multiple issues

### Error Cases
6. Add label to non-existent issue → error
7. Remove non-existent label → error or no-op
8. Invalid label format (if any validation)

### Edge Cases
9. Label with special characters
10. Very long label name
11. Case sensitivity (bug vs BUG)
12. Label on closed issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_labels.rs
- [ ] 12+ test functions
- [ ] Verify label persistence in JSONL export","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenSparrow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:25:51.989260181Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:44:10.369726951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:44:10.369726951Z","close_reason":"Implemented 16 E2E tests for label command in tests/e2e_labels.rs - all tests passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kr5i","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-kvfz","title":"Benchmark baselines + regression thresholds","description":"Define baseline expectations and regression detection for benchmark runs.

## Scope
Establish performance baselines and automated regression detection for br commands.

### Baseline Metrics

**Per Command Group:**
- `list` operations: median time, p95 time, RSS peak
- `search` operations: median time, p95 time for various query complexities
- `create` operations: median time including JSONL flush
- `sync` operations: import/export time for various dataset sizes
- `ready`/`blocked` operations: time including dependency resolution

**Per Dataset Size:**
- Small (100 issues): baseline for quick feedback
- Medium (1,000 issues): typical project size
- Large (10,000 issues): stress testing
- XL (100,000 issues): scale limits

### Statistical Methodology

**Sampling:**
- Minimum 10 runs per measurement (configurable via BR_BENCH_RUNS)
- Warm-up run discarded before measurement
- Cold-start measured separately from warm-start

**Metrics:**
- Median (p50): primary metric for stability
- p95: tail latency for worst-case
- RSS peak: memory high-water mark
- RSS delta: memory growth during operation

**Outlier Handling:**
- Use IQR method: discard values outside [Q1 - 1.5*IQR, Q3 + 1.5*IQR]
- Report outlier count in summary

### Regression Detection

**Thresholds (configurable via env):**
- Time regression: >20% slower than baseline (BR_BENCH_TIME_THRESHOLD=0.20)
- Memory regression: >50% higher RSS than baseline (BR_BENCH_MEM_THRESHOLD=0.50)
- CI threshold: stricter 10% for time (BR_CI=1 mode)

**Comparison:**
- Compare against stored baseline.json
- Report: PASS (within threshold), WARN (5-20% regression), FAIL (>20% regression)
- Generate trend chart data for historical analysis

### Baseline Storage

**Format:**
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "generated_at": "2026-01-20T12:00:00Z",
  "br_version": "0.9.5",
  "br_commit": "abc1234",
  "baselines": {
    "list_small": {"p50_ms": 45, "p95_ms": 62, "rss_mb": 12},
    "list_medium": {"p50_ms": 120, "p95_ms": 180, "rss_mb": 45},
    ...
  }
}
```

**Location:**
- benchmarks/baseline.json (committed to repo)
- Update via: `cargo bench --features baseline-update`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Baseline metrics established for all command groups
- [ ] Regression detection flags >20% time increase
- [ ] Summary table shows pass/warn/fail status per metric
- [ ] CI mode uses stricter thresholds
- [ ] Historical trend data exportable

## Test Coverage (tests/bench_baselines.rs)
- baseline_generation: verify baseline.json created with correct schema
- regression_detection_pass: verify no alert when within threshold
- regression_detection_warn: verify warning at 10-20% regression
- regression_detection_fail: verify failure at >20% regression
- threshold_override: verify BR_BENCH_*_THRESHOLD env vars work
- ci_mode_thresholds: verify stricter thresholds when BR_CI=1
- outlier_filtering: verify IQR outlier removal

## Output Format
```
Benchmark Results (br v0.9.5 @ abc1234)
┌─────────────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────┬────────┐
│ Command         │ p50 (ms) │ p95 (ms) │ RSS (MB)│ Status │
├─────────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────┼────────┤
│ list (small)    │ 42       │ 58       │ 11      │ ✓ PASS │
│ list (medium)   │ 135 (+12%)│ 195     │ 48      │ ⚠ WARN │
│ list (large)    │ 890 (+25%)│ 1200    │ 180     │ ✗ FAIL │
└─────────────────┴──────────┴──────────┴─────────┴────────┘
```

## Logging Requirements
- [BENCH] Running list (medium) - run 1/10
- [BENCH] Run 1: 132ms, RSS 45MB
- [BENCH] Outlier detected: 892ms (>IQR, discarded)
- [BASELINE] list (medium): p50=120ms → 135ms (+12.5%) ⚠ WARN","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkFinch","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:49:26.914745761Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T00:54:57.980077004Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T00:53:04.908824718Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kvfz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kvfz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-owu6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-kvfz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-u8yr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":158,"issue_id":"beads_rust-kvfz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented benchmark regression harness using Criterion baselines and a Python analyzer script. 'scripts/bench_regression.sh' handles baseline management and comparison. 'benches/storage_perf.rs' now respects BENCH_NOISE_THRESHOLD and BENCH_SIGNIFICANCE_LEVEL env vars.","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:53:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-kw8p","title":"Blocking .write.lock acquisition has no timeout or holder diagnostics","description":"Deadlock-surface audit finding: blocking_write_lock falls back from try_lock to file.lock(), which waits indefinitely and does not honor the existing lock-timeout setting or emit holder/path diagnostics beyond a debug log. A live process hung while holding .write.lock (for example during recovery, import, a stuck filesystem call, or terminal/progress I/O) can park every mutating br process forever with no bounded failure mode. Acceptance: add a bounded wait/timeout path for .write.lock, surface actionable diagnostics in CLI/JSON errors, and ensure tests cover a held lock timing out without poisoning the workspace.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T19:00:55.940867813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:00:55.940867813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","deadlock","locking","operator-guidance"]}

A live process hung while holding .write.lock (for example during recovery, import, a stuck filesystem call, or terminal/progress I/O) can park every mutating br process forever with no bounded failure mode. Acceptance: add a bounded wait/timeout path for .write.lock, surface actionable diagnostics in CLI/JSON errors, and ensure tests cover a held lock timing out without poisoning the workspace.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T19:00:55.940867813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:01:34.223401004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:01:34.223066608Z","close_reason":"Added bounded .write.lock acquisition with lock-timeout diagnostics and coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","deadlock","locking","operator-guidance"]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-l7hz","title":"EPIC: Installation & Distribution Automation","description":"# Installation & Distribution Automation

## Background & Rationale

Based on research of mature Rust/Go CLI tools (xf, cass, beads_viewer) and 2025-2026 distribution best practices, br needs a professional-grade installation and update system that makes adoption frictionless and secure.

### Why This Matters
- One-liner installation is the gold standard for CLI tool adoption
- Users expect tools to auto-update (or easily update) themselves
- Multi-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows; x86_64, ARM64) is table stakes
- Security requires checksum verification and signed releases
- AI coding agents benefit from automated tool management

## Goals
Deliver a complete distribution system that enables users to install br with a single command, receive automatic updates, and trust the integrity of downloaded binaries.

## Deliverables

### 1. Multi-Platform Installer Script
- Platform detection (Linux/macOS/Windows, amd64/arm64)
- Download from GitHub Releases with checksum verification
- Fallback to source build if binary unavailable
- Idempotent installation (safe to re-run)
- Lock mechanism to prevent concurrent installs
- Resume capability for interrupted downloads
- PATH modification with shell detection

### 2. Self-Update Command
- `br update` - Check for and install updates
- `br update --check` - Check only, don't install
- `br update --force` - Force reinstall current version
- Use `self_update` or `patchify` crate
- Ed25519 signature verification
- SHA256 hash verification of downloaded files
- Streaming downloads for large files

### 3. Release Automation
- GitHub Actions workflow for multi-platform builds
- Automatic checksum generation
- Release asset naming convention
- Changelog generation from closed issues

### 4. Package Manager Distribution
- Homebrew tap: `brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/br`
- Scoop bucket for Windows users
- AUR package for Arch Linux
- crates.io publishing for Rust users

### 5. Version Management
- Semantic versioning (SemVer)
- `br version` shows build info, git commit, build date
- `br version --check` shows if update available

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single-command installation on all supported platforms
- `br update` successfully updates the binary in-place
- All downloads verified with checksums
- Installation is fully idempotent
- Works behind corporate proxies (HTTPS_PROXY support)

## Technical Approach

### Installer Script Pattern
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash
# OR
curl -fsSL https://br.tools/install | bash
```

### Self-Update with self_update crate
```toml
[dependencies]
self_update = { version = "0.27", features = ["rustls"], default-features = false }
```

### Release Profile (already in place)
```toml
[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"     # Size optimization
lto = true           # Link-time optimization
codegen-units = 1    # Single codegen unit
panic = "abort"      # No unwinding
strip = true         # Remove symbols
```

## Security Considerations
- Ed25519 signatures for release files
- SHA256 checksums in separate .sha256 files
- HTTPS-only downloads (rustls, no OpenSSL)
- Verify before replace (atomic update)

## References
- self_update crate: https://github.com/jaemk/self_update
- patchify crate: https://github.com/danwilliams/patchify
- trust project: CI release builds
- ACFS manifest pattern for tool distribution

## Dependencies
- CI/CD Pipeline (beads_rust-na7)
- version Command (beads_rust-k8p) - completed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SwiftGrove","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:48:13.745544823Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:49.261619258Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:49.261619258Z","close_reason":"Duplicate bead (second- prefix) - original is beads_rust-7nh","external_ref":"second-7nh","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
